steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
# Repair a Regressed CMake Build

I’ve got a small C++ project in `/app` that used to build fine, but after splitting it into `core`, `engine`, `plugin`, and `app`, the CMake setup got messy. The source files themselves are fine; the breakage is in the CMake wiring.

Please fix the build configuration using only `CMakeLists.txt` changes. Don’t edit any `.cpp`, `.h`, or `.in` files.

What I need working again is pretty straightforward from the outside:
- clean out-of-source builds for both `Release` and `Debug`
- clean builds should also work with the `Ninja` generator for both `Release` and `Debug`
- both executables (`myapp` and `selfcheck`) must build and run
- `myapp` should print:

```
text: HELLO
hash: 0x05e918d2
valid: yes
profile: <build type>
```

- `selfcheck` should print:

```
selfcheck: ok (<build type>)
```

The build-profile header should be generated per build type (so Release and Debug runs show the right profile). Also make sure dependency wiring is clean: `core` headers should be exported correctly, and `engine` should declare its dependency on `core` and thread support by using CMake’s Threads package target.

Please keep language standard settings target-scoped (per target compile features) rather than a single global project-wide C++ standard. Concretely:

- Do **not** set `CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD` (or similar) at the top level; remove any project-wide C++ standard if present.
- The **`core`** and **`plugin`** libraries are small and only need **C++17**: add `target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)` and `target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)`.
- The **`engine`** static library and both executables use C++20 features in the existing sources: add `target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)`, `target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)`, and `target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)` so those translation units build as C++20 (this should show up in `compile_commands.json` for `engine/engine.cpp` and `app/main.cpp` when you export compile commands).

Export `core`’s public headers with `target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...)`, and have `engine` link to the `core` target (so include paths propagate). You may split CMake arguments across lines; keep **`core`** and **`PUBLIC`** as the first two arguments to `target_include_directories` for the `core` target.

For threads, in **`engine/CMakeLists.txt`** call `find_package(Threads REQUIRED)` and link **`Threads::Threads`** to `engine` (that satisfies “CMake’s Threads package target”).

One more thing: keep `plugin` as an object-library-style component in the final setup.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@jour-VMware-Virtual-Platform:/app#

Analysis: We are at /app with no prior command output. The task is to repair the CMake wiring across core/engine/plugin/app using only CMakeLists.txt edits: target-scoped C++ standards (core+plugin C++17, engine+executables C++20), proper include propagation from core, engine linking to core and Threads::Threads, plugin kept as an object library component, and correct per-build-type generated header so Debug/Release runs print the right profile. Next step is to inspect the repository structure and current CMakeLists.txt files, then edit them accordingly, then test out-of-source builds for both Debug/Release with both default generator and Ninja, and finally run myapp and selfcheck to validate output.
Plan: 1) Inspect the project tree and all CMakeLists.txt files to understand current targets and where the build regressed. 2) Update top-level and subdirectory CMakeLists.txt to: remove any global C++ standard settings; define core/engine/plugin/app targets correctly; ensure core exports include dirs; engine finds Threads and links to core and Threads::Threads; plugin stays an OBJECT library and is consumed by the executables or engine as intended; and ensure the build-profile header is generated per build type (likely via configure_file with a build-dir output). 3) Run clean out-of-source builds for Debug and Release using default generator and Ninja, with compile_commands exported, and run both executables to confirm required output including correct profile string.

arguments.keystrokes: for f in core/CMakeLists.txt engine/CMakeLists.txt plugin/CMakeLists.txt app/CMakeLists.txt; do echo "\n===== $f ====="; [ -f "$f" ] && sed -n '1,200p' "$f" || echo "(missing)"; done

steps.message:
Analysis: Top-level CMakeLists.txt incorrectly sets a global C++ standard (CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17), which must be removed in favor of target-scoped compile features. Targets are also incorrectly wired: core exports its headers only PRIVATE; engine doesn’t link to core nor Threads; plugin is an OBJECT lib but its headers are PRIVATE; app only builds myapp and links only to engine; selfcheck executable is missing; and the build_profile header generation is not set up (only a .in template exists in app/). We need to fix CMakeLists.txt files to correctly generate per-build-type header, add required targets, and link everything properly.
Plan: 1) Inspect sources briefly to know how targets relate (e.g., who includes build_profile.h, plugin usage, and selfcheck source). 2) Edit top-level CMakeLists.txt: remove global C++ standard, order subdirectories so dependencies are available (core, plugin, engine, app). 3) Fix core: export include dir PUBLIC and add target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17). 4) Fix plugin: keep OBJECT library, export include dir PUBLIC, add target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17). 5) Fix engine: add find_package(Threads REQUIRED), export include dir as needed, link to core and Threads::Threads, add target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20). 6) Fix app: generate build_profile.h into the build tree per build type via configure_file; add include dir for generated header; add selfcheck executable; link executables to engine and include plugin object files if required; add target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20) and same for selfcheck. 7) Test clean out-of-source builds Debug/Release with Unix Makefiles and Ninja, run myapp and selfcheck, and verify compile_commands shows C++20 for engine/app translation units.

Analysis: We have all sources. app/main.cpp includes engine.h, plugin.h, and generated build_profile.h. engine uses <span> and std::thread, so C++20 and Threads linkage are needed. selfcheck.cpp exists and prints build_profile(). Current CMake lacks selfcheck target, lacks build_profile.h generation, has wrong include visibility (core headers PRIVATE), engine doesn’t link core nor Threads, and global C++ standard is set. plugin is already an OBJECT library and should remain so, but its include dirs should be exported properly for consumers.
Plan: Edit only CMakeLists.txt files:
- Top-level: remove CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD settings; add subdirectories in dependency order (core, plugin, engine, app).
- core: make include dir PUBLIC and add target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17).
- plugin: keep OBJECT library, make include dir PUBLIC, add target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17).
- engine: add find_package(Threads REQUIRED); link engine PUBLIC to core and Threads::Threads; set include dir (PRIVATE is fine since headers are in same dir but harmless to set PUBLIC for consumers); add target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20).
- app: configure_file(build_profile.h.in -> build_profile.h in build dir) so it varies per build type; add an INTERFACE target or simply add the binary dir include to both executables; add selfcheck executable; link myapp and selfcheck to engine and to plugin object files (via target_sources(... $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)); add per-target compile features for C++20 on myapp/selfcheck.
Then perform clean out-of-source builds Debug/Release with Ninja (and also default generator), run both executables and verify output/profile.

arguments.keystrokes:
apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: CMakeLists.txt
@@
 cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
 project(CryptoEngine CXX)
-
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
-
-add_subdirectory(app)
-add_subdirectory(core)
-add_subdirectory(engine)
-add_subdirectory(plugin)
+
+# Subdirectories in dependency order
+add_subdirectory(core)
+add_subdirectory(plugin)
+add_subdirectory(engine)
+add_subdirectory(app)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: core/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)
-target_include_directories(core PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: plugin/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)
-target_include_directories(plugin PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: engine/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)
-target_include_directories(engine PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+find_package(Threads REQUIRED)
+
+target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(engine PUBLIC core Threads::Threads)
+target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: app/CMakeLists.txt
@@
-add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
-target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+configure_file(build_profile.h.in build_profile.h @ONLY)
+
+add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
+target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
+target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
+
+add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
+target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
+target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
+target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
+target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

steps.message:
Analysis: Edits did not apply because apply_patch is unavailable, so no CMake changes were made. Builds fail due to missing include paths: engine can’t find core.h because core include dir is still PRIVATE and engine doesn’t link core; app can’t find engine.h because engine include dir isn’t propagated/added. Ninja output shows compilation uses -std=gnu++17, confirming global C++17 is still set at top level. Also jq is not installed, so we should inspect compile_commands.json with grep/sed instead. Next: edit the CMakeLists.txt files using available tools (cat/heredoc or sed) to implement the intended fixes, then rebuild cleanly.
Plan: 1) Overwrite each CMakeLists.txt using cat <<'EOF' heredocs with corrected contents. This avoids needing apply_patch. 2) Reconfigure and rebuild out-of-source Debug/Release (Makefiles) and Ninja Debug/Release. 3) Run myapp and selfcheck from each build to verify output and correct build_profile. 4) Verify C++20 flags for engine/app by grepping compile_commands.json for engine.cpp and main.cpp commands.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(CryptoEngine CXX)

# Subdirectories in dependency order
add_subdirectory(core)
add_subdirectory(plugin)
add_subdirectory(engine)
add_subdirectory(app)
EOF

cat > /app/core/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)

# Export core's public headers
target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/plugin/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)

target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/engine/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)

find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_link_libraries(engine PUBLIC core Threads::Threads)

target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
EOF

cat > /app/app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
configure_file(build_profile.h.in build_profile.h @ONLY)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)

add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: Core/engine wiring and C++20 flags are now correct (compile_commands shows -std=gnu++20 for engine and app). Remaining build failure: app sources include plugin.h but the include path for /app/plugin is not on the compile line. Although plugin exports PUBLIC include dirs, we only add plugin object files via target_sources; that does not propagate include directories. We must add plugin as a dependency for include propagation, e.g., target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE plugin) (works with OBJECT libs for usage requirements) or explicitly add /app/plugin include dir to executables. Best is to link to plugin to propagate its PUBLIC include dirs while still using $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin> to keep object-library style. Then rebuild and run executables to verify outputs in all build variants.
Plan: Update app/CMakeLists.txt to link myapp and selfcheck against plugin (for usage requirements only), while still adding plugin object files via target_sources. Then clean and rebuild Debug/Release and Ninja Debug/Release, and run myapp/selfcheck to confirm required output and correct build_profile per configuration.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
configure_file(build_profile.h.in build_profile.h @ONLY)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine plugin)
target_sources(myapp PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)

add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine plugin)
target_sources(selfcheck PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: Makefiles Debug/Release builds now succeed and both executables run with correct output and correct per-build-type profile. Ninja Debug/Release configuration/build commands were started, but the terminal output got interleaved/truncated; we haven’t yet seen the final ninja build completion nor run outputs for the Ninja builds. Need to re-run Ninja builds cleanly (or continue) and run myapp/selfcheck from /tmp/build-ninja-debug and /tmp/build-ninja-release to confirm they work. Also ensure final requirement: plugin remains OBJECT library (it is).
Plan: 1) Re-run Ninja Debug build (it may already be configured) and build to completion, then run both executables. 2) Configure/build Ninja Release and run both executables. 3) Optionally sanity-check that plugin is OBJECT library and that core include dirs are PUBLIC and engine links Threads::Threads (by grepping the CMakeLists quickly).

steps.message:
Analysis: All required build modes work: clean out-of-source Debug/Release builds succeed with both default (Makefiles) and Ninja generators. Both executables (myapp, selfcheck) build and run, and outputs match the required text including correct per-build-type profile. CMake changes are target-scoped: no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD is set; core/plugin use cxx_std_17; engine and executables use cxx_std_20. core exports headers via target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...). engine finds and links Threads::Threads and links to core so include paths propagate. plugin remains an OBJECT library and is consumed by executables while also linked for usage requirements (include dirs).
Plan: No further changes needed. Mark task complete.
